title: rubus fruticosus subsp. carpinifolius
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. carpinifolius, also known as the bramble, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant, characterized by its unique carpinifolius leaves that resemble those of the hornbeam tree. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. carpinifolius include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antacid, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
